Dear Ms. Gerber: 
Enforcement/CO:1flict of Interest 
3n-6.!~1 
Thank you for your letter of June 4, 1976, requesting 
information as to the contents of a proposed Conflict of 
Interest Code, an explanation as to who should be covered 
by your Code, what deadlines are applicable to development 
of Codes for local government bodies 1 and ,,·:hether minutes 
or digests of hearings are available to the general public. 
The contents of a Conflict of Interest Code are spelled 
out in Government Code §87302, copy attached. 
In addition, 
I enclose a model Conflict of Interest Code dev~loped by the 
Conflicts of Interest Division for use by local school dis-
tricts. 
While you may wish to modify some of its provisions, 
most of the model provisions should be directly applicable to 
your district. 
All "designated employees" must be covered by your 
Conflict of Interest Code. 
Section II of the model Code 
explain's "vho is "vi thin the defini tion of "designated 
employee". 
You might wish to refer to Government Code 
§§82019 and 87302 (a), copy attached, for the sta-tutory 
definition. 
Basically, however, your agency, within the 
strictures of the definition, will decide who should be 
included within the term "designated employee" for your 
agency. 
The deadline for submission of a proposed Conflict of 
Interest Code is set by the code reviewing body. 
For 
school districts with jurisdiction wholly within a county, 
the Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body_ 
For 
districts with jurisdiction in Gore than one county, the 
Fair Political Practices'Commission is the code reviewing 
body. 
You may wish to refer to Government Code §82011, 
copy attached, which dines code reviewing body. 


· Jane Cory Gerber 
June 17, 1976 
Page Tl.·;o 
~'1ith regard to minutes or c.lgests of hearings, some 
local and state agencies have produced transcripts; others 
have produced a sUTILmary of the hearing only, \'lith each 
agency decision identified. 
You might wish to contact 
your county board of supervisors, get a list of the dead-
lines established for local age~2ies, and plan to attend 
one or more of the hearings which are normally held just 
prior to the submission deadlines. 
For your assistance, the following items are enclosed 
with this letter: 
(1) 
The Political Reform Act of 1974 As Amended. 
The Act contains all of the Government Code 
Sections cited above. 
(2) 
A model Conflict of Interest Code for a 
school district (the Rustin Code) 
(3) 
Forms and instructions for filing Statements 
of Economic Interests. 
These instructions and forms, developed for 
Article 2 filers, may be of help to you in 
designing your o~·m for.:cs. 
Your code review-
ing body (probably Board of Supervisors) may 
already have adopted these forms for local 
use. 
You should check directly with your 
board. 
(4) 
A pamphlet entitled: 
Conflict of Interest 
Codes: 
Their Purpose and Preparation with 
attached model code for the mythical BOjai 
Labor Practices commission". 
Finally, you may wish to consult directly with the 
California School Boards Association for further assistance, 
as the Association has devoted considerable time and effort 
on advising its members about their Conflict of Interest 
Code requirements. 
If He 
be of further assistance to you, please let me 
Sincerely, 
EGgar A. Kerry 
Staff Attorney 
Conflicts of Interest 
Division 
(9l6) 322-6444 


14121 Cedarwood Avenue / Westminster, California 92683 
(714) 894.73 
Kenneth E. Ricketts, Ed.D., Superintends 
June 4, 1976 
Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein 
Fa ir Political Practice Commis sion 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Mr. Lowenstein: 
As a member of a committee of management employees who have been requested 
to develop a proposed Conflict of Interest Code as required under Government 
Code 87100, et. seq., I would like to receive from your commission any bulletins 
or opinions as to what this code should contain. Is there any written opinion as to 
ho'w encompassing this policy should be? 
I have spoken with my son, Ken Cory, about this problem and he has indicated 
that lnost of the details for local governrnent bodies are still up in the air. Since 
I am in charge of the purchas ing department for this school district and have been 
asked for input to write a proposed policy, any information from your office would 
be HlOst helpful. 
Has your body 'made any determination at this time as to whether (1) all employees, 
(2) all employees designated as management, (3) only top level executives and 
school board Inembers, or (4) who is covered under this government code section? 
'What deadlines are involved in the developme:lt of codes for local government bodies7 
Are m-iuutes or digests of the various hearings being held available to the general 
public? If so, is it possible to receive copies? 
I am certainly aware of how "bogged down" yOJ and your staff must be at this time. 
Any sugge stj a s or help given me would be dou 
appreciated. 
Sincerely, 
e Cory Ge 
er 
133 1 Edwards Aven~e, WestInin ter, Ca. 92683 (home) 
Phone ~ AC 714 894~7333 ( Purchasing Office Direct) or AC 714 893~9916 (Horne)

sections.question: Thank you for your letter of June 4, 1976, requesting information as to the contents of a proposed Conflict of Interest Code, an explanation as to who should be covered by your Code, what deadlines are applicable to development of Codes for local government bodies, and whether minutes or digests of hearings are available to the general public.
sections.conclusion: All designated employees must be covered by the Conflict of Interest Code as defined in Government Code §87302 and §82019. The deadline for submission is set by the code reviewing body (Board of Supervisors for single-county districts, FPPC for multi-county districts). Hearing minutes or digests vary by agency, and the requester should contact their county board of supervisors and attend hearings to gather information.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks guidance on the required contents of a Conflict of Interest Code, which employees must be covered, applicable deadlines for local government bodies, and whether hearing minutes are publicly available.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that all designated employees must be covered under the Code, submission deadlines are set by the reviewing body, and hearing materials vary by agency. FPPC provides a model Code and supporting materials to assist in development.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (dated June 4, 1976) and FPPC response letter (dated June 17, 1976) from Edgar A. Kerry, Staff Attorney. Question and conclusion sections extracted from FPPC response. High confidence in extraction despite document quality issues.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Thank you for your letter of June 4, 1976, requesting information as to the contents of a proposed Conflict of Interest Code, an explanation as to who should be covered by your Code, what deadlines are applicable to development of Codes for local government bodies, and whether minutes or digests of hearings are available to the general public.

CONCLUSION: All designated employees must be covered by the Conflict of Interest Code as defined in Government Code §87302 and §82019. The deadline for submission is set by the code reviewing body (Board of Supervisors for single-county districts, FPPC for multi-county districts). Hearing minutes or digests vary by agency, and the requester should contact their county board of supervisors and attend hearings to gather information.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a school district administrator's request for guidance on developing a Conflict of Interest Code by providing statutory references, a model code, relevant forms, and directing the requester to the Board of Supervisors as the likely reviewing body.
